channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Crypto spent millions to defeat Sherrod Brown and elect allies. It's ready for a repeat in 2026
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — At a five-star resort tucked in the mountains of Jackson Hole, Wyoming, the cryptocurrency industry was celebrating a historic start to the year on Capitol Hill. Its priorities were sailing through Congress with unusual speed and one senator did not hesitate to say why. Sen.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How malicious party invites target your inbox
item.description: Cybercriminals are using fake invitation emails to trick recipients into downloading malware and stealing personal information and data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hungary vs Armenia live stream: how to watch FIFA World Cup 2026 qualifier online from anywhere
item.description: Here's how to watch Hungary vs Armenia live streams online and for free, as the World Cup qualifier feels like a must-win game for both sides in Budapest.
item.dc:creator: Adam Marshall, TechRadar

item.title: Flying drones take aim at retail theft
item.description: Flying drones could help retailers fight a 93% increase in theft rates as Flock Safety promotes airborne security systems to track suspects and deter crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Apple Mac roadmap for 2025 and 2026 may have leaked – through macOS Tahoe 26
item.description: We could get new MacBook Pros, MacBook Airs, Mac minis, Mac Studios, and iMacs across the next few months.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: ICYMI: the week's 7 biggest tech stories, from Xbox Game Pass controversy to our Wake Up Dead Man review
item.description: The biggest tech news stories from Microsoft, Tag Heuer, Google, Netflix and more for October 11, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Hamish Hector, TechRadar

item.title: Heard kids saying ‘6-7’? It’s so annoying that schools are banning it
item.description: The teaching profession requires a certain degree of patience. Particularly when students discover a new trend to latch onto and repeat at every given opportunity. The latest so-called “brain rot” phrase to flood the classroom: “6-7.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Tesla’s ‘Robotaxi’ service is no Waymo
item.description: Greetings, salutations, and my thanks—as always—for reading Fast Company’s Plugged In. Me: “Are you here just to monitor for safety?” Guy sitting in the driver’s seat of the Tesla Model Y I’m riding in: “I can’t specify.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Hackers leak children’s data in major nursery breach
item.description: Hacker group Radiant stole data from 8,000 children at Kido nursery chain, demanding ransom and directly contacting parents with intimidation tactics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oops - Microsoft Defender issues false end-of-life warning for SQL Server 2017 and 2019
item.description: Microsoft Defender incorrectly reports SQL Server 2017 and 2019 as end-of-life - but don't worry, it's an error.
item.dc:creator: Wayne Williams, TechRadar

item.title: This home battery startup just raised $1 billion to build a new type of power company
item.description: When a winter storm took out the power grid across Texas in 2021, Matt Popovits and his family didn’t have electricity for four days and didn’t have heat in the record cold.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: Google’s Jules coding agent adds CLI, API
item.description: Google Labs has introduced Jules Tools and the Jules API, a command-line interface and API respectively for the Jules coding agent. Jules Tools and the Jules API were announced October 2.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Dominion Voting Systems sold and renamed as Liberty Vote
item.description: Dominion Voting Systems, the voting technology company that was at the center of controversy and false stolen-election claims after President Trump’s 2020 loss, has been sold and will be renamed as Liberty Vote, its new ownership announced on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Army’s NGC2 must be tested in realistic, contested environments
item.description: "What we don't want to do is have a demonstration that goes out there and shows success without proving it in a true environment," Anthony Nigara said. The post Army’s NGC2 must be tested in realistic, contested environments first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Tesla hit with probe after crashes involving a self-driving feature that Musk has boasted about
item.description: Federal regulators have opened yet another investigation into Tesla’s so-called full-self driving technology after dozens of incidents in which the electric vehicle maker’s cars ran red lights or drove on the wrong side of the road, sometimes crashing into other vehicles and endangering drivers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Major Discord hack exposes the real risks of digital ID
item.description: The Discord user data breach offers yet another argument against the UK government’s authoritarian plans for Digital ID. A sensible government would consider the implications before forcing people to risk information with a stunt like this.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Princess of Wales suggests parents should put down the phone to combat social discontent
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Princess of Wales has a suggestion for parents: Please put down the phone. Kate, as she is commonly known, collaborated with adult development researcher Robert Waldinger to warn that technology is contributing to an epidemic of disconnection that is hurting family relationships.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gemini Enterprise is Google’s new ‘front door’ for agentic AI access at work
item.description: Google has introduced a new AI assistant to serve as a platform so users can access and coordinate agents that automate work tasks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Cisco’s Michael Overstreet on securing IT-OT interfaces in federal networks
item.description: Agencies face rising cybersecurity risks as IT and OT converge. Cisco security solutions engineer shares strategies for visibility, segmentation and AI.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Greenland's leader hails EU as trusted friend and urges investment in its minerals
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Greenland’s leader on Wednesday thanked the European Union for being “a trusted friend” and urged the 27-nation bloc to invest in his country’s mineral resources, renewable energy, airports and internet services.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kentucky’s AG launches Roblox lawsuit claiming platform isn’t safe for kids
item.description: Kentucky’s attorney general claimed Tuesday that the online gaming platform Roblox has become a “playground for predators” as he announced a lawsuit accusing the company of lax child safety measures.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Google enhances debugging, performance in Opal, its low-code AI-based app builder
item.description: Google has rolled out new upgrades to Opal, its low-code AI-based application builder, aimed at significantly improving debugging capabilities and its overall performance, as part of its push to streamline AI app development for non-experts.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From pilot to production: How large enterprises can scale XR projects
item.description: Broad adoption of augmented and virtual reality (AR and VR) technologies remains at an early stage, but many businesses are already seeing value in the systems they’ve rolled out: immersive training for frontline employees, for example, or engineers who can collaborate on design using 3D models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 big takeaways from The Hill’s Health Next Summit
item.description: The state of the country’s health care landscape is in flux, according to top health experts and lawmakers, as the federal government shutdown hinges on the battle over health care access and new priorities crystallize under the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Kentucky lawsuit says Roblox fails to protect children on its popular online gaming platform
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Kentucky's attorney general claimed Tuesday that the online gaming platform Roblox has become a “playground for predators” as he announced a lawsuit accusing the company of lax child safety measures.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tesla offers cheaper versions of 2 electric vehicles in bid to win back market share in tough year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Tesla rolled out new, cheaper versions of two of its electric car models on Tuesday in hopes the offerings will help revive flagging sales.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Deloitte to partially refund Australian government for report with apparent AI-generated errors
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Deloitte Australia will partially refund the 440,000 Australian dollars ($290,000) paid by the Australian government for a report that was littered with apparent AI-generated errors, including a fabricated quote from a federal court judgment and references to nonexistent
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
